heroHeadline: Day 45: UK Splits from US on Hormuz Blockade; Australia Signals Naval Role as Western Allied Rift Deepens
heroSubtitle: UK PM Starmer confirmed Britain will not support the US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, creating a public rift among Western allies. Australian PM Albanese called for Hormuz reopening and Australia's Defence chief said ships are 'ready and capable' — signaling Canberra's potential multilateral naval role.
footerNote: Data updated 2026-04-13. Sources include CENTCOM, IDF, IRNA, IAEA, Hengaw, HRANA, Al Jazeera, Anadolu Agency, Reuters, Associated Press, CNN, CNBC, Bloomberg, IEA, DW, The Intercept, Pakistani Foreign Ministry, Washington Post, Financial Times, AgoraVox, The Guardian, BBC, New York Times, Fox News, Military.com, Wall Street Journal, The Independent, Jerusalem Post.
